name: Paraje El Corbinet — Trail run from Cortes de Pallás
name_original: Paraje El Corbinet — Trail run desde Cortes de Pallás
region_intro: Jogging routes Cortes de Pallás navigate a rugged landscape characterized by dramatic mountains and deep ravines in the province of Valencia. The terrain features impressive mountains, including Pico Martés and Muela de Cortes, offering varied elevation changes. The Júcar River carves a significant canyon with steep walls, providing dynamic scenery for running. Trails often follow streams like the Arroyo de Cortes, leading through ravines and past natural water features. Best jogging routes around Cortes De Pallás The most popular jogging route is Paraje El Corbinet — Trail run from Cortes de Pallás, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 45 minutes to complete. This route features an elevation gain of over 130 meters, making it a challenging option. Another top favourite among local runners is Tejador Cave – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás, a difficult 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This running route explores the area around Tejador Cave and El Corbinet Waterfall, offering varied natural scenery. Local runners also love the Views of the Júcar River – Cliffs loop from Cortes de Pallás, a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through the Júcar River canyon, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Jogging around Cortes De Pallás is defined by mountainous terrain, the Júcar River canyon, and streams with waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 5 moderate and 13 difficult running routes available. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 130 runners have used komoot to explore Cortes De Pallás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Cortes de Pallás offers a diverse network of &lt;strong&gt;18 running routes&lt;/strong&gt;. These routes cater to various preferences, with 5 classified as moderate and 13 as difficult, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Cortes de Pallás?
A: The jogging routes in Cortes de Pallás are characterized by a rugged and varied landscape. You'll encounter &lt;strong&gt;dramatic mountains and deep ravines&lt;/strong&gt;, with significant elevation changes. The Júcar River carves a spectacular canyon, providing dynamic scenery, and many trails follow streams like the Arroyo de Cortes, leading through ravines and past natural water features. Some sections may even include old cobbled bridle paths.

Q: Are there running routes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, several running routes in Cortes de Pallás showcase beautiful water features. For instance, the area around the Arroyo de Cortes stream is known for its crystalline pools and waterfalls, including the Cascades of San Andreu and Manantial de San Vicente. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4593119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes with panoramic views of the region?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain around Cortes de Pallás provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking panoramic views. Routes often ascend to areas like the Muela de Cortes, offering vistas of the reservoir and the Júcar River canyon. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River – Cliffs loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the stunning Júcar River canyon. Additionally, the ruins of Chirel Castle offer some of the best views of the canyon and village.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave – El Corbinet Waterfall loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River – Cliffs loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;, providing varied scenery without retracing your steps.

Q: What are some natural landmarks or attractions I can see while running?
A: While running in Cortes de Pallás, you can encounter several natural landmarks. Beyond the waterfalls, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4593119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave&lt;/a&gt;, explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4963542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers&lt;/a&gt;, or even spot the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Caroche&lt;/a&gt;. The Cortes de Pallás Reservoir itself is a picturesque setting, often incorporated into longer routes.

Q: What is a good running route for someone looking for a moderate challenge?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832622298/paraje-el-corbinet-trail-run-from-cortes-de-pallas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paraje El Corbinet — Trail run from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 5.9 km (3.7 miles) long and features an elevation gain of over 130 meters, offering a good workout with beautiful surroundings.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River – Cliffs loop from Cortes de Pallás&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.0 km (13.0 miles) trail with significant elevation changes, often taking over three hours to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Padre Santo loop from Dos Aguas&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 18.4 km long with 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Runners frequently praise the &lt;strong&gt;stunning natural environment&lt;/strong&gt; and the diverse, challenging terrain. The dramatic mountains, deep ravines, and the spectacular Júcar River canyon provide a unique and captivating backdrop for trail running. The variety of routes, from those featuring waterfalls to those with panoramic viewpoints, is also highly appreciated by the komoot community.

Q: Is Cortes de Pallás suitable for trail running year-round?
A: Cortes de Pallás offers a pleasant climate for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal considerations can vary, the region's natural beauty makes it appealing for running throughout much of the year. However, it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially during summer months when temperatures can be high, or in winter for potential colder conditions in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest near the running trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical sites. The ruins of &lt;strong&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, perched on a mountain overlooking the reservoir, offer both historical intrigue and incredible views. The village of Cortes de Pallás itself, with its medieval Islamic layout and narrow, winding streets, provides a unique urban environment that can be incorporated into shorter jogs or explored after a run.

Q: Can I find shaded areas on the running routes in Cortes de Pallás?
A: Many routes, particularly those along streams or near the Cortes de Pallás Reservoir, feature areas with pine trees that provide natural shade. This can be particularly welcome during warmer periods, offering a refreshing break from direct sunlight on more exposed sections of the trails.
tips: The Arroyo de Cortes is a beautiful circular route of about 6 kilometres. Located in Cortes de Pallas, the route passes through beautiful waterfalls, pools of emerald water and numerous bathing areas with crystal-clear water.
